experiment: Aging SRTF + AMCSG compound backtest — Five-Failure Rule integration
hypothesis: Non-preemptive aging-SRTF queue discipline combined with AMCSG optimal variable-c capacity schedule improves SLA-safe goodput/$ vs FIFO+AMCSG
verdict: NULL — aging SRTF + AMCSG indistinguishable from FIFO + AMCSG within noise
root_cause: Running-median live prior (window=200) degenerates aging key to near-constant: predicted_tokens stdev=8.1 vs actual stdev=93.1; only 37 unique predicted values clustered at ~91 tokens. Aging SRTF priority key becomes proportional to aging factor only, collapsing to near-FIFO.

prediction_degeneracy_diagnosis.conclusion: Running-median prior collapses token predictions to near-constant; aging SRTF key becomes aging_factor only; degenerate to near-FIFO

test_results.skipped_reason: main ref not available in CI environment (no_production_module_modified)

integration_value.summary: Integration correctly confirms non-preemptive aging SRTF compiles with variable-c AMCSG loop. Null result is scientifically valid and reproducible.
integration_value.contribution: Eliminates aging-SRTF queue discipline as a viable path under running-median live prior. Points to prediction accuracy as binding constraint.
integration_value.next_path: Accurate per-request token prediction (e.g. Trail/NP-SRPT style) required before queue discipline improvements can clear the OSOTSS frontier.

papers_surveyed.title: Trail: Trace-Informed Adaptive LLM Serving
papers_surveyed.relevance: Per-request token prediction; addresses prediction degeneracy directly

papers_surveyed.title: NP-SRPT: Non-Preemptive SRPT for LLM Inference
papers_surveyed.relevance: Same queue discipline family; requires accurate prediction
